programme.title: O termómetro
programme.desc: Centrado na realidade social galega, o programa ofrece unha ampla cobertura en directo da actualidade e, da man de expertos, aborda os temas que máis preocupan os espectadores.

programme.title: Mira ti
programme.desc: Programa que ten como cometido descubrir o porto de Vigo. Para iso, cinco reporteiras achegarán reportaxes con historias, datos e curiosidades para amosar esta mega estrutura que fai de Vigo un dos portos máis importantes de Europa.

programme.title: Galicia noticias
programme.desc: Toda a información e última hora de Galicia, do resto de España e do mundo desde unha perspectiva galega.

programme.title: Atrápame se podes
programme.desc: Cinco concursantes competirán cada día neste concurso de preguntas e respostas de cultura xeral. A través de catro fases, os participantes loitarán para gañar importantes premios e o bote acumulado.

programme.title: Hora galega
programme.desc: David Espiño e Rocío Durán, acompañados dun equipo de reporteiros espallados por todo o país, contan as historias cotiás que están ocorrendo en Galicia. A cultura popular, os barrios, as súas xentes e o bo humor xúntanse cada tarde.

programme.title: Heicho cantar queridiña
programme.desc: Programa musical polo que pasarán artistas de todo o mundo para interpretar versións das cancións máis variadas. Realizarase unha análise en clave humorística a través dos sons, costumes, tradicións e tópicos sobre Galicia.

programme.title: No bico un cantar
programme.desc: A través dunha canción, realizamos unha viaxe que permite coñecer a nosa historia a través da música, con opinións de expertos en diferentes áreas do coñecemento, entrevistas a pé de rúa e documentación audiovisual de arquivo.

programme.title: Libro de familia
programme.desc: Retrato de tres familias galegas que pertencen a distintos estratos sociais e residen en Santalla: os Cabana, labradores e propietarios dun ultramarinos, conviven cos Lamela, saga de caciques que ten contas pendentes cos Freire.

programme.title: Parlamento
programme.desc: Programa que informa da actividade política de Galicia, do funcionamento e das últimas noticias do Parlamento de Galicia.

programme.title: Bos días Fin de semana
programme.desc: Emisión especial da fin de semana do espazo informativo máis madrugador da Televisión de Galicia. Toda a actualidade da semana con conexións en directo cando os acontecementos o requiren.

programme.title: Gaña a túa aldea
programme.desc: Tres participantes deben responder preguntas sobre as nosas bisbarras. A dinámica do concurso consiste en atinar entre tres respostas para ir avanzando comarca a comarca ata chegar a un punto de destino. Na rolda final preséntase un peto de diñeiro.

programme.title: Galicia noticias fin de semana
programme.desc: Chus Tanoira achéganos as noticias máis destacadas da semana e as últimas novidades da actualidade nacional e internacional.

programme.title: No Foco
programme.desc: Reportaxes en profundidade para achegarnos de forma rigorosa a asuntos de interese social que están de actualidade.

programme.title: Galicia bonita
programme.desc: Un equipo de expertos formado por unha arquitecta, unha decoradora e albaneis teñen o reto de converter en bonito os feísmos máis comúns das vivendas galegas nun prazo de cinco días.

programme.title: Meus
programme.desc: Programa que fai un retrato das familias galegas, sexan clásicas, monoparentais, extensas, multirraciais ou sen lazos de sangue.

programme.title: Onde vai a xente
programme.desc: 'Onde vai a xente' achéganos á nosa realidade para vivir as pequenas historias de cada día, da man de Alfonso Tellado, xubilado da Bazán, e de profesión galego retranqueiro como el mesmo se presenta.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again, but will they take on board their veteran's advice as they serve their `Feast To Say Thank You'?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran who's worked at some of London's toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury' and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six' in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS'. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Selin puts her own unique twist on tea and toast. James creates a decadent coconut parfait accompanied by mango puree filled doughnuts for his dessert `Tree of Life' inspired by the coconut palm tree, the NHS and both of their contributions to our health. Scott hopes his elevated gypsy tart `Honey Bee Good' will secure him a place in the final.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their four-course menus again, but will they take on board their veteran's advice as they re-make their `Feast To Say Thank You'?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Vying to be crowned champion of Scotland are three talented newcomers to the GBM kitchen. Lorna McNee is a former Scottish Chef of the Year and is renowned for her technical precision which she has gained from working her entire career at 2 Michelin-starred restaurant Andrew Fairlie at Gleneagles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and the chefs are all out to impress. The heat is increased as all three serve their unique takes on lobster. Ross is hoping to snatch victory with his dish `Lobster Aid'. Lorna's `Live Well, Live Very Well' features only three elements, each of which she needs to deliver perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ross is hoping to impress with `Oh Deer It's Time For Your Penicillin', his tribute to Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming featuring an unusual yeast element as well as venison. Across the kitchen, Lorna, a former Game Chef of the Year, is also cooking venison in a bid to impress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's dessert day and the last chance for the chefs to impress before one is sent home. Ross is attempting to elevate a comfort classic into a dish worthy of the banquet. Lorna's cooking a personal tribute to the NHS staff who have cared for her during treatment for a rare blood condition, using unusual yuzu and tonka bean flavours. Once again Ben is serving an experimental dish with his `Dance for the Greater Good' which includes a bees wax ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs compete in the heat for the central region. Returner Ryan Simson-Trotman, who failed to reach the national finals last year, is originally from Nuneaton and opened his own restaurant Orwells in the Oxfordshire countryside with his partner Liam, who is also a contestant in the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and the chefs are pulling out all the stops. Marianne pays tribute to the `angels' in her local hospital who looked after her during a recent illness. Ryan's dish is a reference to the purported health benefits of fish high in omega 3 oils. While Sabrina is inspired by her aunt, a retired nurse who used to take in food to share with her fellow workers during her shifts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and following yesterday's results, tension is high. Both Marianne and Ryan are cooking muntjac in the hope of impressing the veteran judge, while Sabrina is making an ambitious technical take on a family feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With one dish left to impress the veteran judge, the chefs cook their desserts. Marianne is looking to raise a smile with her `carry on' themed dessert, complete with surprise presentation. Sabrina hopes to make it through with an elevated take on jelly and ice cream while Ryan goes all out with his very sweet treat `A Spoonful of Sugar'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining central region chefs do battle for a place in the national finals. They must impress the formidable panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton with their four course menus.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andrew is attempting to pay tribute to NHS staff with his innovative take on a traditional healing recipe, `Mum's Flu Fighting Chicken Soup'. Jason's `A Tea From The Heart' is a play on a post-war remedy which harks back to the era when the NHS was formed. And Chris is hoping to impress with his dish inspired by a therapeutic hospital garden featuring foraged herbs from the Welsh valley where he lives including wild chervil, pickled pine buds and yarrow tips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After the starter results, the chefs return to the kitchen hoping for high scores with their fish dishes. Chris once again is using ingredients he has foraged from around his restaurant, while Jason hopes to impress with a spicy scallop dish and Andrew prepares a cod dish with 11 different elements.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are pulling out all the stops in a bid for big scores with their mains. All three dishes pay tribute to the founder of the NHS Aneurin Bevan, Chris's `Everything But The Squeal' is an ambitious pork sharing platter, while Jason's `For The Rich And The Poor' showcases lamb, and Andrew is hoping to impress with `Aneurin Bevan For Better Steak'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and the last opportunity for the chefs to impress. Chris is hoping to triumph with another dish which showcases the foraged ingredients available around his restaurant, while Jason attempts a technical feat in his dessert inspired by a childhood hospital stay and Andrew is inspired by a dish by his great grandmother used to cook. Which two will make it through to the judging chamber?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Wales regional final as the two remaining chefs do everything they can to impress the judging panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. After tensions ran high in the week, the chefs are under pressure to deliver perfection. The judges are joined by Aneira Thomas, the first baby born on the NHS, who is named in tribute to the service's founder Aneurin Bevan and who went on to dedicate her life to the service. Who will win a place in the national finals?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the turn of three chefs representing the South West, including two who have competed before but meet in the kitchen this week for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and with Tom renowned as a fish specialist all eyes are on him. He's cooking his take on a classic British fish supper in tribute to a charity which looks after the health of local fishermen. Jude is attempting a complex fusion dish featuring four different fish and an unusual X Ray themed presentation, while Olivia is paying tribute to the staff who looked after her during the birth of her first son with her take on curried monkfish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the main course and two of the chefs are hoping duck will win the day. Tom is preparing a technically challenging duck dish steeped in NHS history while Jude is making a multi-element Chinese duck dish inspired by his mum. Olivia is hoping her dish, using two unusual cuts of beef, will score top points. Olivia also visits her local children's hospital, home of the second busiest paediatric A&E in the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With just one more chance to impress their veteran judge, the chefs are all hoping to use their pastry skills to deliver technically complex desserts. Tom's cooking an `Invalid Fruit Tart' inspired by a 1950s recipe, while Jude is making six different apple elements for his `An Apple A Day'. Olivia is using stout in reference to an outdated old wives' tale which suggested the drink was a healthy source of iron during pregnancy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's time for the two remaining chefs representing the South West to go head to head as they cook their menus again for the exacting panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. With a spot in the National Finals at stake, both are aiming for perfection as they bid for glory. The judges are joined by Barbara Childs, a Matron in the Cardiac Intensive Care Unit at Great Ormond Street Hospital, who isn't afraid to speak her mind in the chamber.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs take to the kitchen in a bid to represent the North West in the national final. They include Ellis Barrie, from the award winning Marram Grass in Anglesey, Liam Simpson-Trotman, an accomplished pastry chef and co-owner of the 4 AA rosette Orwell's in Oxfordshire, and Craig Sherrington, who sharpened his skills working under top names including Eric Chavot and is now Chef Patron of Virginia House in Cumbria.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the fish course and the chefs are all gunning for tens. Craig pays tribute to a friend who has revolutionised hospital food in Morecambe Bay, while Liam is inspired by health advice his grandma gave him and Ellis is dedicating his dish to the staff who delivered his son Albert. With cod, mackerel, shrimp and cockles on the menu, which of the chefs will score highly?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Time for the chefs to cook their mains and the pressure is on. Craig is hoping to impress using two cuts of Dexter beef, while Liam prepares his take on a Bajan lamb stew and Ellis prepares four cuts of suckling pig. Liam meets his personal hero, Nola Ishmael OBE, who became the UK's first ever black Director of Nursing.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the dessert course and the last chance for the North West chefs to impress in a bid to make it through to the regional final. Liam is cooking `Not So Hot Toddy' in honour of a friend who works nightshifts in the NHS, while Ellis' `Worth Their Weight in Gold' is dedicated to the staff who saved his brother's life and Craig's dish `The Missed Celebration' pays tribute to his wife with an unusual Christmas concept.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the week return to cook their menus again. Judging them are Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver who are joined by Dr Jake Dunning MBE, an infectious disease specialist who has been recognised for his work during the West Africa Ebola epidemic.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs are doing battle to represent Northern Ireland. Tommy Heaney, Head Chef at the Great House Hotel in South Wales is taken on by Shauna Froydenlund, Chef Patron at Marcus Waring's two Michelin starred restaurant and James Devine, the 2017 National Chef of the Year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course, the chefs return to the kitchen to cook their fish dishes. James is making his elevated take on a fried chicken takeaway as a tribute to the NHS staff who care for his sister. Shauna is also taking inspiration from a personal connection with a tribute to staff from a facility in her hometown of Londonderry. Tommy is hoping to impress with a dish dedicated to the discovery of DNA.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two courses down it's time for the chefs to cook their main courses. It's the battle of beef with each using the same meat in their innovative creations. Tommy also meets the first baby ever born on the NHS.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's dessert course and the final chance for the chefs to impress. After such a tightly fought battle all week, each is hoping to deliver a technically perfect dessert. Tommy and James are both using passion fruit in their complex dishes while Shauna is hoping to use her pastry chef training to score full marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs representing Northern Ireland go head to head. Judging as ever are Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined by Shehan Hettiaratchy, lead trauma surgeon at St Mary's Hospital who helped treat victims of the Grenfell Tower fire.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs from all 8 regions must now battle it out for the honour of a spot on 2018's `Feast To Say Thank You' banquet menu, celebrating 70 years of the NHS, one of Britain's most prized institutions.Each day the regional champions will be cooking one of their courses again for the discerning panel of judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but after some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the starter round, everyone is desperate to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. Once again the chefs present their innovative, witty or heart warming creations, with lobster, mackerel, John Dory, turbot and hake among the dishes being scrutinized.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. Both chefs and judges alike are amazed at the high standard of cooking and an incredible number of 10s are dished out as the chefs serve up venison, rabbit, duck, beef, lamb and pork for scrutiny.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's dessert day and with just one place remaining at the banquet feast, each chef is giving everything they've got for a chance to say thank you to the heroic NHS staff. All the chefs have significantly re-worked or re-invented their desserts since the regional heats, proving that they'll do everything they can in a bid for that final winning spot.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's dessert day and with just one place remaining at the banquet feast, each chef is giving everything they've got for a chance to say thank you to the heroic NHS staff. All the chefs have significantly re-worked or re-invented their desserts since the regional heats, proving that they'll do everything they can in a bid for that final winning spot.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the grand finale as the winning chefs cook their dishes at a banquet for the heroes of the NHS. Having arrived at St Bartholomew's Hospital, the chefs are shown the incredible location for event, the almost three hundred year old Great Hall.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for the top spot are 3 new chefs to the competition representing some of the most exciting culinary talent from London, Paul Walsh, Luke Selby and Ben Marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Only two will proceed to Friday's judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London chefs must cook their four course menus again. The chefs need to impress the formidable judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs new to the competition compete in the North East heats. Three of the region's chefs battle it out for the topspot: Mike Carr, Samira Effa, and Tom Anglesea. The three chefs must present the menu of their careers, inspired by 50 years of British pop music.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through their heat and will serve up their main courses and desserts. The stakes are high and again they are each serving a unique take on the pop music brief. Mike is confident about his northern dish, `Rack n Roll', which puts both classic Yorkshire hogget and traditional Lancashire hotpot in the spotlight.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again for the judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the British pop industry's finest.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomer Kray brings an urban edge to proceedings, with a starter inspired by Grime Music called `Fire in the Booth'. It combines hot sauce, veal sweetbreads and tuna belly. Returner Sabrina is compiling a dish of her favourite ingredients, including pasta and ox cheek, for her Italian inspired starter called `My Mixtape'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For his main course, Ryan Simpson-Trotman is confident his dish, `The UK's Number 1', will hit the top spot with his combination of a beef pie and rump cap. Sabrina Gidda is taking inspiration from her grandad's collection of old 45s for her `Sunday Jukebox' main: pork Wellington and Black Country faggots.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central chefs must cook their four course menus again for judges food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for a place on the banquet menu are two chefs returning to the competition for a second time, plus a supremely talented newcomer. Liam Simpson, who co-owns a high-end gastropub near Henley-on-Thames, is hoping to make it all the way after being knocked out in the heats during the 2018 competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through the regional heats and, as the lowest scoring chef will be eliminated from the competition at the end of the show, the pressure is on. They start by cooking their all-important main courses, the centrepiece of the banquet menu. Michelin-starred newcomer Hrishikesh Desai is pushing himself to the limit with a complex multi-part venison dish, inspired by the bands which play at the Kendal Calling music festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the north west cook their dishes head to head for a panel of expert judges, in a bid to secure themselves a place in the national finals. Will they have acted on the advice given by the veteran judge earlier in the week, and learn from their mistakes?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotland's finest chefs do battle for a chance to cook at the banquet celebrating 50 Years of Popular Music. Gordon Jones, the only newcomer, faces returners Lorna McNee and Ben Reade, who are going head-to-head for the second time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and desserts, each battling to produce smash hit dishes that wow their veteran. For her main, Lorna is confident her lamb dish `With A Little Help From My Friends', dedicated to supportive friends, is a celebratory dish fit for the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs must cook their four courses again, but this time they'll need to appeal to four new palates: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver plus a celebrity guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emily Scott is the chef proprietor of Cornwall's renowned `St Tudy Inn'. Her two rivals are from across the sea on Jersey. Joe Baker is largely self-taught, but at just 28 already running his own restaurant, `No 10', in St Helier. A neighbouring restaurant is Michelin starred `Samphire' where Lee Smith is head chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emily's David Bowie inspired dish celebrates Britain's food heroes and she's using Cornish duck meat from her local producer that she pairs with grilled radicchio and Malbec sauce. Joe has sourced his ingredients locally too, using hay from his family farm and Jersey beef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs need to impress the esteemed judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the pop stars the banquet will celebrate. They are joined by one half of the synthpop duo The Communards, Reverend Richard Coles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom `Westy' Westerland is a former National Chef of Wales and head chef of the Brasserie at Lucknam Park in Wiltshire. Cindy Challoner is the former head chef at prestigious teaching restaurant `The Classroom' in Cardiff. They face Andy Sheriden formerly of Sosban restaurant in Llanelli, returning to the competition for his second attempt at making it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andy cooks venison in celebration of his favourite band, Oasis. He accompanies the loin with rhubarb and beetroot ketchup, black pudding purée, pickled beetroot and a pistachio crumb. Westy's beef dish `The Cow Shed' is named after a former stage at the Glastonbury festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from Wales must cook their four course menus again. They must take on board their veteran's advice as they serve their tributes to Britain's music success.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs begin by cooking their starters for a surprise veteran of the competition. Chris' dish `Brixton Academy Through the Years' is his refined take on the classic lamb, post gig junk food he ate after nights out at Brixton Academy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chris is cooking his interpretation of the classic beef dish Tournedos Rossini using guinea fowl. His inspiration is Proms in the Park and the British artists who mix classical and modern music styles. Alex's dish commemorates his late brother and the song by Northern Ireland artist DonnaTaggart that he associates with the difficult time of his death.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from Northern Ireland will recreate their four course menu for the esteemed judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver. There's also a guest judge, one of the pop stars the banquet will celebrate.

Paranormal 911
programme.desc: A correctional officer witnesses the Grim Reaper collect the soul of a dead inmate from a prison. Documentary drama about apparent paranormal encounters.

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters
programme.desc: A young woman fights a battle with the demons that threaten to kill her. Anorexia and the grisly apparition of an old hag have plagued her nightly for six years, leaving her dangerously sleep deprived, and emotionally scarred.

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters
programme.desc: Whether it's a levitating little brother or a poltergeist hurling things at you, living in a haunted house can be a terrifying experience.

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters
programme.desc: The macabre ghosts of murder victims are particularly disturbing, and those shocking visions deeply affect both Anthony and Heather.

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters
programme.desc: Three friends experience the supernatural in different ways, with different responses to the strange things they've seen.

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters
programme.desc: A new neighbourhood is plagued by poltergeists after an Indian burial ground is disturbed, but it's not just the spirits that are restless, as Marc makes a gruesome discovery of his own in an episode about living beside the unburied dead.

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters
programme.desc: Rose goes to work and finds herself in a disturbing environment, straight out of a gothic romance, while Barbara, as a young burlesque dancer, discovers the supernatural secrets of an old theatre.

programme.title: Ghostly Encounters
programme.desc: Ron's dead mother comes back from the grave to visit him in his home, while the ghost of Pam's mum surprises her grieving daughter, in an episode about just how deep a mother's love goes.

programme.title: Paranormal Survivor
programme.desc: Terrifying real-life encounters with the supernatural. Normal people are plagued by entities, who lay claim to their possessions.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Edwardian Britain by visiting Cardiff, which grew rich selling the coal that powered the Empire. Alice hears the stories of the Edwardian miners and their backbreaking and often dangerous work.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the English Civil War through the history of Oxford, the city at the heart of a battle between an autocratic king and his rebellious parliament.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al Murray is joined by legendary master of stand-up, Anuvab Pal, to explore the truth about Britain's rocky relationship with India.

programme.title: Shipwrecks: When History Resurfaces
programme.desc: Kevin Sempé dives in search of wrecks harking back to the Roman Empire's greatness. The journey begins with the Nave Romana.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A team discovers clues to Scotland's first kingdoms, metal detectorists unearth a hoard of Viking treasure, and a new housing development reveals a graveyard of Iron Age warriors.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Finds include the lost WWI training trenches on Salisbury Plain, Britain's first `double henge', discovered just down the road from Stonehenge, where the evidence suggests our ancestors feasted and made sacred offerings as part of a visit to the ritualistic Stonehenge landscape, and luxury foreign goods discovered at Tintagel, the legendary childhood home of King Arthur.

programme.title: Britain's Secret Islands
programme.desc: An exploration of Rathlin Island, including a visit to a Neolithic stone tool factory, a unique upside-down lighthouse, and a WWII shipwreck.

programme.title: Britain's Secret Islands
programme.desc: A trip through England's south coast, diving with blue sharks, exploring the battlements of Drake's Island, learning the origins of Scouts, and checking out fossils on the Isle of Wight.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores Manchester's history of protest and revolution during the Industrial Revolution, from Marx to the Suffragettes and the Peterloo massacre.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Roman Britain by studying the history of Chester, Britain's most Roman town.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on his most curious walk yet, across the ancient and turbulent kingdom of Northumbria. In the seventh century, at the height of the Dark Ages, the rule of Northumbria stretched from Edinburgh to York.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson travels along the wonderful seascapes of the North Norfolk coast and through one of the least developed spots in the country.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores 2018's best archaeological finds from the east of the UK: a monument as old as Stonehenge, a dig at the site of Britain's first tank battle, and some disturbing Roman burials.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts follows the excavation of Iron Age Britain's most spectacular grave. A team of archaeologists in East Yorkshire have uncovered the remains of only the third upright chariot burial ever found in Britain.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comedian Loyiso Gola embark on a road trip through South Africa to explore the UK's imperial history in the country, from colonial wars to the deals that eventually led to the development of apartheid.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comic Kevin Kropinyeri reveal that Captain James Cook was not really a captain, explore the Aboriginal history of Australian cricket, and uncover the often brutal land grabs that impacted Indigenous Australians.

programme.desc: From the Indian Ocean's clear blue atolls to the icy waters of the St. Lawrence River, an epic expedition with the Cinémarine team to capture the remains of six of the world's most spectacular underwater disasters.

programme.title: Shipwrecks: When History Resurfaces

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a one-bedroom flat in Catford, London, a large house near the River Mersey and a semi-detached property in Alvaston in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Sutton Coldfield near Birmingham, a derelict house in a rural village near Dover in Kent and a basement without planning permission in Ipswich in Suffolk are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 20-year-old first-time developer, a banker turned developer and a builder who wants to create a new home for his soon-to-be-wife all buy properties at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion are in Chesterton, Hornchurch and Sudbrook to hear their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A run-down listed village house in Farningham in Kent is given the wow factor while houses in Great Barr in Birmingham and Crewe in Cheshire are also given great makeovers. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and catch up on their progress after the transformations.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy talks to a pastor and his wife who plan to build their dream eco house in Brompton in Kent. Meanwhile, Martin meets a first-time developer in Wolverhampton, and Dion looks at a huge building which already comes with a tenanted flat and a hair salon in Newcastle-under-Lyme.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A commercial and residential building in Beeston in Nottinghamshire, a four-storey house in East Dulwich in London and a flat in Godalming in Surrey are all given high-end makeovers. Will the developers, who all bought at auction, make a profit?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Hatfield in Hertforshire, a flat in Croydon and a workshop and land in Heanor in Derbyshire are sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the owners and hear their plans to make money from their bricks and mortar.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mum and dad buy a piece of land in Stevenage in Hertfordshire in the hope of developing a pair of houses for their children, a couple who buy a top-floor flat in Motherwell in Scotland want to make a profit on the rent, and two young brothers buy a run-down house in South Normanton.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Langton Green in Kent is given a high-end refurbishment and extension, a father-and-son team cleverly refigure the existing space in a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and two lifelong friends have high hopes for a piece of land they buy behind a house they already own in Northwood in Middlesex.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Leamington Spa in Warwickshire, a terraced house in London's Plumstead and a workshop on the Isle of Sheppey in Kent are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin hear about the plans for the three properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up on ambitious plans to redevelop the site of a former school in Ebbw Vale in Wales, while Dion Dublin talks to a first-time developer planning to extend up and out at a semi in Bloxwich in the West Midlands.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Sutton in London, a studio apartment in Chatham in Kent and a terraced house in Middlesbrough are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Biggin Hill with huge potential, a centuries-old cottage in a beautiful part of Devon and an end terrace crying out for an upstairs bathroom are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell joins the team, and looks at an old mews house in an historic part of Litchfield in Staffordshire, while Martin Roberts assesses the potential of a bungalow in Hanley in Stoke-on-Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks at a large terraced house in Dover in Kent with bags of potential. Martin Roberts is in Camborne in Cornwall viewing a house with lots of issues. Dion Dublin meets a couple who bought a flat in Woodford Green in Essex.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell is in Gillingham in Kent meeting a couple embarking on their first renovation development, Martin Roberts talks to a man with ambitious plans for a house in London's Chingford, and Dion Dublin looks round a semi in Wolverhampton.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell hears development plans for a two-bedroom flat in Godalming in Surrey, Martin Roberts looks at a house in a terrible state of repair in Telford in Shropshire, and Dion Dublin sizes up the work needing to be done at a terraced house in Smethwick in the West Midlands.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell hears development plans for a two-bedroom flat in Godalming in Surrey, Martin Roberts looks at a house in a terrible state of repair in Telford in Shropshire, and Dion Dublin sizes up the work needing to be done at a terraced house in Smethwick in the West Midlands.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat with great sea views in Margate in Kent, a bungalow in Whaley Thorns in Nottinghamshire and an end terrace in Longbridge in Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A listed building in Brentwood in Essex dating back to the 16th-century is sold under the hammer, along with a flat in Halesowen in Birmingham and an end terrace in Northwood in Stoke-on-Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large semi in New Barnet in London is bought as an ambitious project to turn into a family home, a family do up a semi in Dover in Kent to make a profit, and two friends club together to try their hand at refurbishing a terraced house in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large Victorian semi packed full of original features in Dresden in Stoke-on-Trent, a two-bed semi in Croydon in London and some public loos in Kincardine in Fife are sold at auction. Martin, Dion and Martel meet the new owners and hear their plans for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A huge 1930s house in Kidderminster in Worcestershire, a purpose-built flat in a 1980s block in Wembley in North London, and a substantial Victorian semi in Burslem in Staffordshire are sold at auction.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Folkestone in Kent, a terrace in Hanley in Stoke-on-Trent and a huge detached house in Seascale in Cumbria are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear their plans for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house with period features in Forest Gate in London, a house with a stream but close to a railway line in Handsacre in Staffordshire, and a large three-storey property in Plymouth are all sold at auction.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A third-floor flat in Walworth in London, a piece of land in Derbyshire's Chesterfield, and a block of flats in Hengoed in Mid Glamorgan are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a house in Guildford, Martin Roberts falls in love with a Grade II-listed house in Torquay in Devon, and Dion Dublin is in Barry in Wales assessing all the work needed on a mid-terrace house. Will the developers' plans run smoothly?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel talks to a 20-year-old developer whose 4th property is in Madeley Heath, Dion meets two friends refurbishing a flat in Birkenhead, and Martin talks to identical twin brothers who have ambitious plans for a derelict fire station in Paisley.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Callington in Cornwall, a three-bed semi in Willenhall near Wolverhampton and a terraced house in Hertfordshire's Watford are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Dion and Martel meet the new owners, and hear their plans for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander talks to experienced developers who deliver a stunning renovation on a subsidence-troubled semi in Coulsdon in Surrey, Dion Dublin meets two brothers with ambitious plans for a commercial property in Derby, and Martel Maxwell meets with two golfing friends turned developers who plan to turn round a one-bedroom flat in Telford in Shropshire in record time.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bed detached house ripe for extending in Bilston near Wolverhampton, a cosy cottage in Nettlestead Green in Kent and a terraced house in Etruria in Stoke-on-Trent without a bathroom or an inside loo are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A garage with development potential in Margate in Kent, a ground-floor flat in a Victorian house in Shepherd's Bush, London, and a piece of land ripe for building on in Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The trio look at a family home in Kent, a former bar in Llanelli in Wales and a terrace in Croydon, all sold at auction. Will any of the developers make a return on their investments?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom semi in Crewe, a top-floor flat in London and a terraced house in Oldham are sold under the hammer. All the owners are happy with the prices they paid, but will any of them be able to turn a profit at the end of the projects?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: While developers in Croydon in Surrey and Donnington in Shropshire wrestle with the question of whether to lose a bedroom each for a better placed kitchen or bathroom, two friends in Derby consider creating two more in their six-bedroom house.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a rundown cottage in Probus in Cornwall, a father buys a flat in Mitcham in Surrey as a long-term investment for his young daughter and a seasoned property developer buys a house in Tunstall in Stoke-on-Trent for her daughter.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is in Plaistow in London looking around a three-bedroom semi, Lucy is checking out a small piece of land in Crayford in Kent, and Martin meets a father and son who have bought a small house with a big garden in Wheelock in Cheshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former miner's cottage in Aberaman in South Wales and semis in Adderley Green in Stoke and Streatham in South London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion look around auction buys in Birmingham, Oldham and Gravesend and discover what the new owners plan to do with their investments.

programme.title: Diabolical: Deadly Love
programme.desc: When three women die of the same mysterious illness, the hunt is on to prove their deaths were no accident. Can detectives unravel this twisted tale of deceit?

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: A Christian couple's weekend getaway takes a tragic turn when they are found murdered on a northern California beach. Satanic imagery found nearby leads local Sheriff's deputies on a manhunt complicated by a cagey killer who covered his tracks.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: A woman is murdered the same night she's supposed to go out with rising star, Ashton Kutcher. Police believe the clues point to a serial killer. As the number of victims climbs, one brave woman manages to survive. But can she identify her attacker?

programme.title: Still a Mystery
programme.desc: The callously dismembered body of a missing woman turns up in trash bags.

programme.desc: A war veteran turns up dead, and a missing man leaves a creepy voicemail.

programme.desc: When Olivia marries Christopher Jones, a correctional officer, he runs his home like a prison. On February 14, a day made for love turns into a day of horror, and a home security system, helps detectives realise all is not what it seems.

programme.title: Til Death Do Us Part
programme.desc: Ashlee and Emmett Corrigan seem to have it all, including true love, five beautiful children and a growing business. However, an explosive encounter blows their lives apart in an instant, revealing their marriage was not what it seemed.

programme.title: Twisted Love
programme.desc: A man becomes fixated on his co-worker, believing they should be married. The problem is, she's already married to a jealous husband.

programme.desc: A high society lady ensnares men in her deadly web and sucks the life right out of them. Later, a marriage for wealth falls apart, leading to a shocking murder.

programme.desc: A stalker's obsession with a girl puts a family in grave danger, leading to a killing spree.

programme.title: Death by Fame

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: A Christian couple's weekend getaway takes a tragic turn when they are found murdered on a northern California beach. Satanic imagery found nearby leads local Sheriff's deputies on a manhunt complicated by a cagey killer who covered his tracks.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: A woman is murdered the same night she's supposed to go out with rising star, Ashton Kutcher. Police believe the clues point to a serial killer. As the number of victims climbs, one brave woman manages to survive. But can she identify her attacker?

programme.title: Still a Mystery
programme.desc: The callously dismembered body of a missing woman turns up in trash bags.

programme.title: Still a Mystery

programme.title: Til Death Do Us Part
programme.desc: Ashlee and Emmett Corrigan seem to have it all, including true love, five beautiful children and a growing business. However, an explosive encounter blows their lives apart in an instant, revealing their marriage was not what it seemed.

programme.title: Twisted Love
programme.desc: A man becomes fixated on his co-worker, believing they should be married. The problem is, she's already married to a jealous husband.

The challenges are tougher, the environment is harsher, and they will be pushed to their limits and tested like never before.

programme.title: ITV Racing Live LIVE
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents live coverage of racing from Sandown, Leicester and Haydock. Today's feature meeting of the day is at Sandown where the 2025/2026 Jumps season concludes with some top-class action including the Grade 1 Celebration Chase. Plus, there's flat racing from Leicester and Haydock. Analysis from Sir AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reporting from Alice Plunkett and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles.

programme.title: The Floor
programme.desc: From 81 players down to the final nine - after weeks of fierce competition, it all comes down to this. In the grand finale of the game show hosted by Rob Brydon, the remaining contestants face one last series of high-stakes duels - two players will compete in a best-of-three final, but only one of them will conquer The Floor and take the 50,000-pound jackpot home.

programme.desc: Continuing his travels around France, James visits a region known for its traditional hearty cuisine, buried treasures and a love of all things duck - the Dordogne.

programme.desc: Kenny Toal presents a round-up of all the news in the region. Followed by a local weather forecast.

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show with Mel Blatt, Michelle Visage, and Seann Walsh all trying to guess well-known phrases from animated clues - if they can 'say what they see', they could get the chance to win.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: The dealers den pitches up in Solihull, where Simon Schneider gets into a movie poster shoot out. Plus, Fay Rutter eyes up a gent's Rolex and Henry Nicholls spends cash to not own some saucy postcards as well as an emotional end to the auction.

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: The fast-paced game of words, hosted by Adil Ray, where three pairs of contestants face Lingo grids, fiendish puzzlewords, and a tense head-to-head round as they battle to reach the final and a chance to win a 15,000-pound jackpot - trying to Lingo all the way this time are brother and sister Penny and Steve, sisters Allandah and Miah, and couple Alison and Mick.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the show where four contestants take on one of the country's finest quiz brains as Greg, Gemma, Rachel and Reuben think they have what it takes to beat a Chaser and take home thousands of pounds.

programme.desc: The latest headlines from around the world. Followed by a national weather forecast.

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a brand-new series of `I'm A Celebrity...' where nine of the most memorable camp mates from previous series battle it out in trials to decide who will become the first-ever I'm A Celebrity Legend, but this time they've swapped the Australian jungle for the South African bush. The challenges are tougher, the environment is harsher, and they will be pushed to their limits and tested like never before.

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Not wasting any time, Graham suspends the neighbours seven metres high in the first immunity challenge that gets them all in a spin, with revelations about each other they'd perhaps rather not air in public. Which household can snatch safety and save themselves from the first vote? And who is going to find themselves in the firing line early on?

programme.title: Skyscraper
programme.desc: Will Sawyer is a former FBI agent and U.S. war veteran who now assesses security for skyscrapers. While he's on assignment in China, the world's tallest and safest building catches on fire -- and Will gets framed for it. Now a wanted man and on the run, he must find those responsible, clear his name and somehow rescue his family members when they become trapped inside the inferno.

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region and pays a visit to the de La Rochefoucauld family.

programme.title: Jurassic Park - The Lost World
programme.desc: Sequel in which the entrepreneur who genetically engineered the dinosaurs in Jurassic Park transpires to have cloned more of the prehistoric monsters on another island location. Researchers arrive to observe the creatures in their natural habitat, but hot on their heels is a band of mercenaries intent on capturing the dinosaurs and transporting them back to San Diego Zoo.

programme.desc: Raymond Blanc journeys to the northernly tip of Scotland and the majestic Castle of Mey that was once home to Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother.

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Six households live side by side, competing in a high-stakes reality game for a cash prize.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: Minority Report
programme.desc: A detective works for a police department that uses three infallible psychics to help convict murderers before they have actually committed their crimes. When the detective discovers that he has been identified as a future killer, he becomes a fugitive from his own colleagues.

programme.title: English Football League Highlights
programme.desc: Action from across the Football League, featuring West Brom v Ipswich, Norwich v Swansea, Sheffield United v Preston and Stoke v Portsmouth.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Floor
programme.desc: From 81 players down to the final nine - after weeks of fierce competition, it all comes down to this. In the grand finale of the game show hosted by Rob Brydon, the remaining contestants face one last series of high-stakes duels - two players will compete in a best-of-three final, but only one of them will conquer The Floor and take the 50,000-pound jackpot home.

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.

programme.title: Fern Britton: Inside the Vet's
programme.desc: Fern meets Lotta, a German Shorthaired Pointer puppy who has bitten off more than she can chew and helps vet James with Badger, a handsome but nervous moggie who is in for a routine vaccination.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: Continuing his travels around France, James visits a region known for its traditional hearty cuisine, buried treasures and a love of all things duck - the Dordogne.

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Britain's weekday breakfast show on the latest news, weather and sports as well as offering information on entertainment, health and money.

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Featuring a generous morning dose of celebrity interviews, showbiz updates, lifestyle tips and everything else relevant to start the day in an upbeat manner.

programme.title: ITV Lunchtime News
programme.desc: The latest headlines from around the world. Followed by a national weather forecast and all the news and weather in your region.

programme.title: ITV News Central
programme.desc: Early evening news from around the Midlands. Followed by Central weather.

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show with Mel Blatt, Michelle Visage, and Seann Walsh all trying to guess well-known phrases from animated clues - if they can 'say what they see', they could get the chance to win.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: The dealers den pitches up in Solihull, where Simon Schneider gets into a movie poster shoot out. Plus, Fay Rutter eyes up a gent's Rolex and Henry Nicholls spends cash to not own some saucy postcards as well as an emotional end to the auction.

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: The fast-paced game of words, hosted by Adil Ray, where three pairs of contestants face Lingo grids, fiendish puzzlewords, and a tense head-to-head round as they battle to reach the final and a chance to win a 15,000-pound jackpot - trying to Lingo all the way this time are brother and sister Penny and Steve, sisters Allandah and Miah, and couple Alison and Mick.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the show where four contestants take on one of the country's finest quiz brains as Greg, Gemma, Rachel and Reuben think they have what it takes to beat a Chaser and take home thousands of pounds.

programme.title: ITV News Central
programme.desc: Early evening news from around the Midlands. Followed by Central weather.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A Party Election Broadcast on behalf of the Labour Party.

programme.title: ITV Evening News
programme.desc: The latest headlines from around the world. Followed by a national weather forecast.

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a brand-new series of `I'm A Celebrity...' where nine of the most memorable camp mates from previous series battle it out in trials to decide who will become the first-ever I'm A Celebrity Legend, but this time they've swapped the Australian jungle for the South African bush. The challenges are tougher, the environment is harsher, and they will be pushed to their limits and tested like never before.

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Not wasting any time, Graham suspends the neighbours seven metres high in the first immunity challenge that gets them all in a spin, with revelations about each other they'd perhaps rather not air in public. Which household can snatch safety and save themselves from the first vote? And who is going to find themselves in the firing line early on?

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a brand-new series of `I'm A Celebrity...' where nine of the most memorable camp mates from previous series battle it out in trials to decide who will become the first-ever I'm A Celebrity Legend, but this time they've swapped the Australian jungle for the South African bush. The challenges are tougher, the environment is harsher, and they will be pushed to their limits and tested like never before.

programme.title: ITV News at Ten and Weather
programme.desc: Includes a national weather forecast and the latest news and weather in your region.

programme.title: ITV News Central
programme.desc: Early evening news from around the Midlands. Followed by Central weather.

programme.title: Skyscraper
programme.desc: Will Sawyer is a former FBI agent and U.S. war veteran who now assesses security for skyscrapers. While he's on assignment in China, the world's tallest and safest building catches on fire -- and Will gets framed for it. Now a wanted man and on the run, he must find those responsible, clear his name and somehow rescue his family members when they become trapped inside the inferno.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Murder Line
programme.desc: Tensions explode at the Heron and an unexpected arrival leads to a deadly confrontation - as mayhem unfolds and Henry fights to regain control, a last-minute intervention changes everything in the end.

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region and pays a visit to the de La Rochefoucauld family.

programme.title: Jurassic Park - The Lost World
programme.desc: Sequel in which the entrepreneur who genetically engineered the dinosaurs in Jurassic Park transpires to have cloned more of the prehistoric monsters on another island location. Researchers arrive to observe the creatures in their natural habitat, but hot on their heels is a band of mercenaries intent on capturing the dinosaurs and transporting them back to San Diego Zoo.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is back in the kitchen on a Saturday morning where he's cooking up a couple of delicious recipes for singer Michael Ball and enjoying dishes from chefs Galton Blackiston, Ruth Hansom and Glynn Purnell.

programme.title: Raymond Blanc's Royal Kitchen Gardens
programme.desc: Raymond Blanc journeys to the northernly tip of Scotland and the majestic Castle of Mey that was once home to Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother.

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: An action-packed, sun-soaked and food-filled road trip across the USA. The next leg of the celebrated chef's journey around the world to explore the food, people and places that inspire him.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: ITV Racing Live LIVE
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents live coverage of racing from Sandown, Leicester and Haydock. Today's feature meeting of the day is at Sandown where the 2025/2026 Jumps season concludes with some top-class action including the Grade 1 Celebration Chase. Plus, there's flat racing from Leicester and Haydock. Analysis from Sir AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reporting from Alice Plunkett and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles.

programme.desc: Look back at some of the most dramatic end games in Tipping History, including agonising trades and jackpot counters teetering on the edge.

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show with Mel Blatt, Michelle Visage, and Seann Walsh all trying to guess well-known phrases from animated clues - if they can 'say what they see', they could get the chance to win.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: The dealers den pitches up in Solihull, where Simon Schneider gets into a movie poster shoot out. Plus, Fay Rutter eyes up a gent's Rolex and Henry Nicholls spends cash to not own some saucy postcards as well as an emotional end to the auction.

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: The fast-paced game of words, hosted by Adil Ray, where three pairs of contestants face Lingo grids, fiendish puzzlewords, and a tense head-to-head round as they battle to reach the final and a chance to win a 15,000-pound jackpot - trying to Lingo all the way this time are brother and sister Penny and Steve, sisters Allandah and Miah, and couple Alison and Mick.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the show where four contestants take on one of the country's finest quiz brains as Greg, Gemma, Rachel and Reuben think they have what it takes to beat a Chaser and take home thousands of pounds.

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a brand-new series of `I'm A Celebrity...' where nine of the most memorable camp mates from previous series battle it out in trials to decide who will become the first-ever I'm A Celebrity Legend, but this time they've swapped the Australian jungle for the South African bush. The challenges are tougher, the environment is harsher, and they will be pushed to their limits and tested like never before.

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Not wasting any time, Graham suspends the neighbours seven metres high in the first immunity challenge that gets them all in a spin, with revelations about each other they'd perhaps rather not air in public. Which household can snatch safety and save themselves from the first vote? And who is going to find themselves in the firing line early on?

programme.title: Skyscraper
programme.desc: Will Sawyer is a former FBI agent and U.S. war veteran who now assesses security for skyscrapers. While he's on assignment in China, the world's tallest and safest building catches on fire -- and Will gets framed for it. Now a wanted man and on the run, he must find those responsible, clear his name and somehow rescue his family members when they become trapped inside the inferno.

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region and pays a visit to the de La Rochefoucauld family.

programme.title: Jurassic Park - The Lost World
programme.desc: Sequel in which the entrepreneur who genetically engineered the dinosaurs in Jurassic Park transpires to have cloned more of the prehistoric monsters on another island location. Researchers arrive to observe the creatures in their natural habitat, but hot on their heels is a band of mercenaries intent on capturing the dinosaurs and transporting them back to San Diego Zoo.

programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the show where four contestants take on one of the country's finest quiz brains as Greg, Gemma, Rachel and Reuben think they have what it takes to beat a Chaser and take home thousands of pounds.

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a brand-new series of `I'm A Celebrity...' where nine of the most memorable camp mates from previous series battle it out in trials to decide who will become the first-ever I'm A Celebrity Legend, but this time they've swapped the Australian jungle for the South African bush. The challenges are tougher, the environment is harsher, and they will be pushed to their limits and tested like never before.

programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Not wasting any time, Graham suspends the neighbours seven metres high in the first immunity challenge that gets them all in a spin, with revelations about each other they'd perhaps rather not air in public. Which household can snatch safety and save themselves from the first vote? And who is going to find themselves in the firing line early on?

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa

programme.title: Skyscraper
programme.desc: Will Sawyer is a former FBI agent and U.S. war veteran who now assesses security for skyscrapers. While he's on assignment in China, the world's tallest and safest building catches on fire -- and Will gets framed for it. Now a wanted man and on the run, he must find those responsible, clear his name and somehow rescue his family members when they become trapped inside the inferno.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Murder Line

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region and pays a visit to the de La Rochefoucauld family.

programme.title: Jurassic Park - The Lost World
programme.desc: Sequel in which the entrepreneur who genetically engineered the dinosaurs in Jurassic Park transpires to have cloned more of the prehistoric monsters on another island location. Researchers arrive to observe the creatures in their natural habitat, but hot on their heels is a band of mercenaries intent on capturing the dinosaurs and transporting them back to San Diego Zoo.

Which household can snatch safety and save themselves from the first vote? And who is going to find themselves in the firing line early on?

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a brand-new series of `I'm A Celebrity...' where nine of the most memorable camp mates from previous series battle it out in trials to decide who will become the first-ever I'm A Celebrity Legend, but this time they've swapped the Australian jungle for the South African bush. The challenges are tougher, the environment is harsher, and they will be pushed to their limits and tested like never before.

programme.title: ITV News at Ten and Weather
programme.desc: Includes a national weather forecast and the latest news and weather in your region.

programme.title: ITV News Granada Reports
programme.desc: All the latest news, sport and breaking stories from around the North West.

programme.title: Skyscraper
programme.desc: Will Sawyer is a former FBI agent and U.S. war veteran who now assesses security for skyscrapers. While he's on assignment in China, the world's tallest and safest building catches on fire -- and Will gets framed for it. Now a wanted man and on the run, he must find those responsible, clear his name and somehow rescue his family members when they become trapped inside the inferno.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Murder Line
programme.desc: Tensions explode at the Heron and an unexpected arrival leads to a deadly confrontation - as mayhem unfolds and Henry fights to regain control, a last-minute intervention changes everything in the end.

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region and pays a visit to the de La Rochefoucauld family.

programme.title: Jurassic Park - The Lost World
programme.desc: Sequel in which the entrepreneur who genetically engineered the dinosaurs in Jurassic Park transpires to have cloned more of the prehistoric monsters on another island location. Researchers arrive to observe the creatures in their natural habitat, but hot on their heels is a band of mercenaries intent on capturing the dinosaurs and transporting them back to San Diego Zoo.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is back in the kitchen on a Saturday morning where he's cooking up a couple of delicious recipes for singer Michael Ball and enjoying dishes from chefs Galton Blackiston, Ruth Hansom and Glynn Purnell.

programme.title: Raymond Blanc's Royal Kitchen Gardens
programme.desc: Raymond Blanc journeys to the northernly tip of Scotland and the majestic Castle of Mey that was once home to Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother.

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: An action-packed, sun-soaked and food-filled road trip across the USA. The next leg of the celebrated chef's journey around the world to explore the food, people and places that inspire him.

programme.title: ITV Racing Live LIVE
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents live coverage of racing from Sandown, Leicester and Haydock. programme.desc: The fast-paced game of words, hosted by Adil Ray, where three pairs of contestants face Lingo grids, fiendish puzzlewords, and a tense head-to-head round as they battle to reach the final and a chance to win a 15,000-pound jackpot - trying to Lingo all the way this time are brother and sister Penny and Steve, sisters Allandah and Miah, and couple Alison and Mick.

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region and pays a visit to the de La Rochefoucauld family.

programme.title: Jurassic Park - The Lost World
programme.desc: Sequel in which the entrepreneur who genetically engineered the dinosaurs in Jurassic Park transpires to have cloned more of the prehistoric monsters on another island location. Researchers arrive to observe the creatures in their natural habitat, but hot on their heels is a band of mercenaries intent on capturing the dinosaurs and transporting them back to San Diego Zoo.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is back in the kitchen on a Saturday morning where he's cooking up a couple of delicious recipes for singer Michael Ball and enjoying dishes from chefs Galton Blackiston, Ruth Hansom and Glynn Purnell.

programme.title: Raymond Blanc's Royal Kitchen Gardens
programme.desc: Raymond Blanc journeys to the northernly tip of Scotland and the majestic Castle of Mey that was once home to Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother.

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: An action-packed, sun-soaked and food-filled road trip across the USA. The next leg of the celebrated chef's journey around the world to explore the food, people and places that inspire him.

programme.title: ITV Racing Live LIVE
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents live coverage of racing from Sandown, Leicester and Haydock. Plus, there's flat racing from Leicester and Haydock. Analysis from Sir AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reporting from Alice Plunkett and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.desc: The fast-paced game of words, hosted by Adil Ray, where three pairs of contestants face Lingo grids, fiendish puzzlewords, and a tense head-to-head round as they battle to reach the final and a chance to win a 15,000-pound jackpot - trying to Lingo all the way this time are brother and sister Penny and Steve, sisters Allandah and Miah, and couple Alison and Mick.

programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a brand-new series of `I'm A Celebrity...' where nine of the most memorable camp mates from previous series battle it out in trials to decide who will become the first-ever I'm A Celebrity Legend, but this time they've swapped the Australian jungle for the South African bush. programme.title: The Neighbourhood
programme.desc: Not wasting any time, Graham suspends the neighbours seven metres high in the first immunity challenge that gets them all in a spin, with revelations about each other they'd perhaps rather not air in public. programme.title: The Only Way Is Essex
programme.desc: Gatsby encourages Dean to clear the air with Chloe Sims after not hearing from her since their date but then Dean discovers Chloe spent the weekend with a younger man! Amber realises why Dan is refusing to commit to her. Trying to help, Yaz suggests a relationship counsellor for Dan and Amber. Arg learns that he needs to face the consequences of his actions, and Jordan faces the wrath of Myles after his efforts to comfort a freshly single Courtney blows up in his face.

programme.title: The Only Way Is Essex
programme.desc: Arg's behaviour pushes Gemma to breaking point and Jordan's friends give him the cold shoulder. Myles gives Courtney an ultimatum while Dan struggles to commit to Amber. Chloe has doubts about Adam and Tommy is thrown a birthday party fit for a king.

programme.title: The Only Way Is Essex
programme.desc: Georgia organises a couples' trip and whisks Tommy away to Italy to escape work. To help boost his spirits Georgia arranges a surprise in the shape of millionaire hottie Sam Mucklow. Meanwhile, back in Essex, Courtney is working on her revenge body.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the iconic game show as Coronation Street legend Simon Gregson goes head-to-head with the infamous Banker - supported on the wings by his family, friends and a few famous faces, Simon must choose the right boxes and resist the Banker's tempting cash offers until just the right moment in order to take home a fortune for his chosen charity.
